steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a vocabulary-in-context question testing precise word choice. The sentence must complete the idiom “can be ______ to how they are pronounced in Yoruba” in a way that expresses attribution to an origin — the idea that Puerto Rican vowel sounds derive from Yoruba. The passage has just established that African languages “made important contributions” to the Puerto Rican dialect.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — traced: “can be traced to” is a standard idiom meaning “can be attributed to its origin in” — exactly the linguistic influence the passage describes. ✓
  • Choice B — surrendered: means to hand something over on demand; “can be surrendered to” implies giving up rather than originating from, which is nonsensical in this context. ✗
  • Choice C — announced: means officially declared or proclaimed; the passage discusses linguistic inheritance, not a public declaration about pronunciation. ✗
  • Choice D — offered: means presented as a gift; “can be offered to” implies the Puerto Rican pronunciation is being given to Yoruba, reversing the direction of influence. ✗
That is the correct answer.
